requirement_ids: BMS-SEC-SEN-001; BMS-SEC-SEN-002; BMS-SEC-SEN-003; BMS-SEC-COM-001; BMS-SEC-COM-002; BMS-SEC-COM-003
validation_coverage: SENS; PLAUS; CROSS; REPLAY; SRC; CAN
required_evidence_outcome: Rejected or inconsistent measurement data cannot alter trusted state; the exact input, verdict, and reasons are retained.

validation_coverage: CMD-001..006; CMD-101..112; communication replay and source tests
required_evidence_outcome: An unauthorized, stale, or invalid-state command is rejected; simulated state and evidence are preserved.
Each diagnostic-service decision records the session, security level, role, operating-state preconditions, sequence result, and modeled response.

requirement_ids: BMS-SEC-FW-001; BMS-SEC-FW-002; BMS-SEC-FW-003; BMS-SEC-FW-004; BMS-SEC-FW-005a; BMS-SEC-FW-005b; BMS-SEC-FW-006
required_evidence_outcome: An invalid, incompatible, downgraded, modified, or interrupted software package cannot reach execution; verification and recovery outcomes are recorded.
Unauthorized, incompatible, or internally inconsistent configuration is rejected; every approved protected change is audited.
Timeout, abnormal-rate, malformed-input, latency, and bounded-resource behavior are reproducible, and the defined operating-mode-dependent safe response is recorded.
An unauthorized BESS request is rejected according to identity, authentication, role, network zone, function, register range, or rate policy.
Complete, tamper-evident, hash-chained evidence supports reproduction, finding creation, remediation, passing retest, and accountable closure.
Unauthorized debug, bootloader, or console access is rejected and logged; authorized lifecycle access is authenticated and audited.
